thesis.supertrend: AI factory buildout is expanding from chips into full-system deployment, stressing packaging, testing, power, and cooling.
thesis.lane: HBM test and advanced packaging support tools
thesis.thesis_sentence: Before AI factory capacity scales cleanly, the supporting test and inspection layers have to scale first.
thesis.bottleneck_call: HBM test throughput and advanced-packaging inspection are still meaningful gating layers.
thesis.weakest_assumption: AI-related packaging and test spending remains durable instead of fading into a short inventory cycle.
thesis.thesis_breaker: If packaging expansion slows or HBM demand cools materially, the support-tool layer loses urgency.

bottlenecks.label: HBM test enablement
bottlenecks.why_it_matters: HBM output still needs the right test throughput and yield support before it becomes usable supply.

bottlenecks.label: Advanced-packaging inspection
bottlenecks.why_it_matters: As AI packaging gets more complex, inspection systems become more important before scale-up.

sources.title: Q1 2026 results
sources.body: FormFactor reported record DRAM revenue and said HBM application demand increased. Management also said the company is shipping in production to all three major HBM manufacturers.

sources.title: March 2026 Hawk order announcement
sources.body: Camtek announced approximately $25 million of Hawk systems orders tied to AI applications from an IDM customer. The release supports the view that advanced packaging inspection tools are benefitting from AI-related demand.

sources.title: Q1 2026 results
sources.body: Amkor reported record first-quarter revenue and highlighted demand related to AI-driven advanced packaging. The company also pointed to continued expansion in Arizona.

sources.title: NVIDIA DSX platform
sources.body: NVIDIA presents DSX as a blueprint for full AI factory infrastructure rather than a single server product. The framing pushes research attention toward the broader deployment stack.
